input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

`AppThemeBinding` lets any property react to the system theme automatically.

### XAML — Before (hardcoded light)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="White">
  <VerticalStackLayout>
    <Label Text="Welcome"
           TextColor="Black"
           FontSize="24" />
    <Label Text="Subtitle"
           TextColor="#444444" />
    <Button Text="Continue"
            BackgroundColor="#6200EE"
            TextColor="White" />
  </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

### XAML — After (theme-aware)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">
  <VerticalStackLayout>
    <Label Text="Welcome"
           TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}"
           FontSize="24" />
    <Label Text="Subtitle"
           TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />
    <Button Text="Continue"
            BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
            TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=Black}" />
  </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Each `AppThemeBinding` takes a `Light` and `Dark` value. The framework switches automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

### Tip: Use App-level resources to avoid repetition

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
  <Color x:Key="PageBg"
         x:DataType="Color"
         />
  <!-- Can't use AppThemeBinding directly on Color, so use styles: -->
  <Style x:Key="ThemedPage" TargetType="ContentPage">
    <Setter Property="BackgroundColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}" />
  </Style>
  <Style x:Key="PrimaryLabel" TargetType="Label">
    <Setter Property="TextColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />
  </Style>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- In your page -->
<ContentPage Style="{StaticResource ThemedPage}">
  <Label Text="Welcome" Style="{StaticResource PrimaryLabel}" />
</ContentPage>
```

### C# alternative — `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme<T>`

For color properties there's a convenience method:

```csharp
// Equivalent to BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}"
page.SetAppThemeColor(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,          // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E") // dark
);

label.SetAppThemeColor(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Colors.White
);
```

For **non-color** properties, use the generic version:

```csharp
// e.g. swap an image source by theme
image.SetAppTheme<string>(
    Image.SourceProperty,
    "logo_light.png",  // light
    "logo_dark.png"    // dark
);
```

### Detecting / overriding the current theme in C#

```csharp
// Read the current theme
AppTheme current = Application.Current.RequestedTheme;

// Force a specific theme (overrides system setting)
Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;

// Reset back to follow the system
Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Unspecified;
```

**Key points:**
- `AppThemeBinding` is reactive — it updates live when the user toggles their OS theme.
- Define themed styles in `App.xaml` to keep pages clean.
- Use `SetAppThemeColor` in C# for colors, `SetAppTheme<T>` for everything else.
- Set `UserAppTheme` to let users override the system default within your app.
